item.title: Gas shortages and policy failures
item.description: ONCE, gas was considered an almost inexhaustible resource in Bangladesh. People used to dry clothes over gas stoves, keep burners running all day and pay only a nominal monthly bill. Illegal connections were, in many cases, not even considered a serious social offence, because there was a firm belief that this gas would never run out. But that belief has now been shattered......
